title: Daily Stand-up Updates
definition: In IT and agile teams, a daily stand-up update usually covers three points: what you did yesterday, what you'll do today, and any blockers.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.line: Yesterday, I completed the login module and fixed two bugs in the payment flow.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.line: Today, I'll start working on the API integration for the dashboard.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.line: I don't have any blockers at the moment.

dialogue.speaker: Teammate
dialogue.line: I'm currently blocked on the staging environment access. Can someone help?

practicePhrases: This task is on track / slightly delayed.

commonMistakes.wrong: Yesterday I am working on login page.
commonMistakes.right: Yesterday I worked on the login page.
commonMistakes.why: Use Past Simple for completed work from yesterday, not Present Continuous.
Use 'don't have any' rather than 'have no' in casual spoken updates, and 'blockers' is usually plural.

quiz.question: Which correctly reports yesterday's work?
options: Yesterday I am completing the task.
quiz.explanation: Use Past Simple for finished actions from yesterday.

quiz.question: Today, I ___ (plan) to start the API integration.
quiz.explanation: Present Simple describes today's plan in a stand-up.
